name: behavior_prefer_mcp_tools
when: Working in an IDE with MCP server extensions, or when guideai MCP tools could replace CLI/API interactions.
steps: Check available tools: Review `MCP_SERVER_DESIGN.md` for the catalog (`behaviors.*`, `runs.*`, `compliance.*`, `actions.*`, `bci.*`, `raze.*`, `amprealize.*`).
Prefer MCP over CLI/API: MCP provides consistent schemas, automatic telemetry, and cross-surface parity.
Leverage IDE extensions: VS Code and other IDEs with MCP support can invoke tools directly for real-time behavior retrieval, run status, and compliance validation.
Record usage: Cite MCP tools in action logs for reproducibility.
Fallback gracefully: If MCP unavailable, use CLI commands with same parameters.
Report gaps: Document missing MCP equivalents in `docs/capability_matrix.md`.

name: behavior_use_raze_for_logging
when: Adding logging to any service, debugging production issues, implementing telemetry, or replacing ad-hoc print statements.
steps: Import: `from raze import RazeLogger` or `from raze import RazeService`.
Configure sink: TimescaleDB (production), InMemory (tests), JSONL (local).
Include context fields: `run_id`, `action_id`, `session_id`, `actor_surface`.
Use structured fields: `logger.info("Request processed", endpoint="/v1/users", latency_ms=45)`.
For VS Code/web, use the `RazeClient` TypeScript wrapper.

name: behavior_use_amprealize_for_environments
when: Provisioning development environments, managing containerized resources, setting up test infrastructure.
steps: Check if Amprealize is needed (container orchestration, compliance) or simpler Docker Compose suffices.
Create/select blueprint from `packages/amprealize/src/amprealize/blueprints/`.
Use plan/apply/destroy workflow: `amprealize plan --blueprint <name>`, review, then `amprealize apply`.
Configure hooks for ActionService/ComplianceService when audit trails required.
Monitor via `amprealize status`, clean up with `amprealize destroy`.

name: behavior_extract_standalone_package
when: Adding functionality that could be reused across projects, or refactoring tightly-coupled code.
steps: Evaluate reusability: Is it generic enough to benefit other projects?
Follow Raze/Amprealize pattern:

name: behavior_prevent_secret_leaks
when: Initializing repos, preparing commits/pushes, wiring CI pipelines.
steps: Confirm `.gitignore` excludes secrets directories/files.
Ensure `pre-commit` is installed via `./scripts/install_hooks.sh`.
Run `scripts/scan_secrets.sh` before PRs; remediate immediately.
Record `guideai scan-secrets` action with sanitized reports.

name: behavior_rotate_leaked_credentials
when: Secrets, keys, or credentials appear in code, logs, or chat.
steps: Remove leaked artifact from repo; ensure `.gitignore` blocks future commits.
Instruct user to rotate affected credentials per `SECRETS_MANAGEMENT_PLAN.md`.
If secret reached git history, document scrub steps (`git filter-repo`).
Replace production secrets with placeholders in `.env.example`.

name: behavior_unify_execution_records
when: Work involves run persistence, SSE updates, CLI status, or execution records.
steps: Inventory all execution record definitions and storage adapters.
Align fields with RunService contract (`MCP_SERVER_DESIGN.md`), ActionService payloads (`ACTION_SERVICE_CONTRACT.md`).
Route mutations through canonical RunService/ActionService APIs.
Validate state transitions across Web/CLI/API/MCP surfaces.

name: behavior_align_storage_layers
when: Modifying UnifiedStorage, JSON/SQLite/Firestore adapters, PostgresPool.
steps: Check for duplicate methods or mismatched field names.
Normalize signatures per `AUDIT_LOG_STORAGE.md` and `REPRODUCIBILITY_STRATEGY.md`.
Verify PostgresPool commits before returning connections.
Update schema docs and indexes.
Test across at least two backends.

name: behavior_externalize_configuration
when: Encountering hardcoded file paths, ports, Firebase configs, API keys.
steps: Add typed config entries via `config/settings.py`.
Load from env vars/`.env` with safe fallbacks per `SECRETS_MANAGEMENT_PLAN.md`.
Update Docker Compose, manifests, `.env.example`.
Remove hardcoded values; fail fast with descriptive errors if missing.

name: behavior_harden_service_boundaries
when: Code makes loopback HTTP calls, uses inline API keys, or crosses service boundaries inconsistently.
steps: Determine if call should be in-process or external client.
For in-process, use direct service calls honoring contracts.
For cross-service, configure URLs/credentials, add auth guards, log failures.
Add integration tests.

name: behavior_curate_behavior_handbook
when: Updating behavior definitions, prompts, retrieval metadata, OR processing behavior proposals.
steps: Review existing entries: to avoid duplicates—search by name and keywords.
For new behaviors: Follow the Behavior Proposal Template (see Behavior Lifecycle section).
Include clear triggers: Specific conditions that activate this behavior.
Define validation steps: How to verify the behavior was applied correctly.
Assign appropriate role: Student (routine), Teacher (examples), Strategist (novel).
Calculate confidence score: Based on historical validation (0.8+ for auto-approve).
Update BehaviorService index: Run `python scripts/seed_behaviors_from_agents_md.py`.
Update retrieval metadata: Ensure keywords enable semantic search discovery.
Add regression tests: Create test cases in `tests/test_behavior_*.py`.
Log in BUILD_TIMELINE.md: Document with date and brief rationale.

name: behavior_sanitize_action_registry
when: Touching action registry schemas, defaults, or multi-tier storage.
steps: Keep registry modules inside package tree.
Ensure default URLs match `ACTION_REGISTRY_SPEC.md`.
Provide graceful fallbacks per `REPRODUCIBILITY_STRATEGY.md`.
Add tests for resolution order and CLI/API parity.

name: behavior_instrument_metrics_pipeline
when: Telemetry events, dashboards, or metrics contracts need updates.
steps: Map against `TELEMETRY_SCHEMA.md`, `MCP_SERVER_DESIGN.md` MetricsService.
Ensure events carry run IDs, behavior refs, token accounting for PRD metrics.
Update Kafka topics, warehouse schemas, retention notes.
Add automated validation checks.

name: behavior_wire_cli_to_orchestrator
when: Implementing or modifying CLI commands controlling runs.
steps: Map CLI to RunService/ActionService/BehaviorService per `MCP_SERVER_DESIGN.md`.
Support key ops with clear args per `ACTION_REGISTRY_SPEC.md`.
Add Click tests including CLI/API/MCP parity.
Ensure output references unified run IDs.

name: behavior_lock_down_security_surface
when: Adjusting CORS, auth middleware, secrets/API keys.
steps: Restrict CORS via config with safe dev defaults.
Audit endpoints for consistent auth.
Remove inline secrets per `SECRETS_MANAGEMENT_PLAN.md`.
Add security tests.

name: behavior_update_docs_after_changes
when: Any behavior changes developer setup, API contracts, or UX flows.
steps: Update `README.md`, `PRD.md`, `WORK_STRUCTURE.md`, `BUILD_TIMELINE.md`.
Regenerate API reference if schemas shift.

name: behavior_prototype_consent_ux
when: Designing or updating consent experiences across Web/CLI/IDE.
steps: Review `docs/AGENT_AUTH_ARCHITECTURE.md` and `docs/CONSENT_UX_PROTOTYPE.md`.
Reference scope catalog entries with purpose/expiry/obligations.
Define telemetry for prompt impressions, approvals, denials.
Run WCAG AA accessibility checks.

name: behavior_handbook_compliance_prompt
when: Starting a task, resuming after pause, or when user requests handbook adherence assurance.
steps: Walk through compliance checklist before executing.
Reference behaviors in plan.
Reconfirm after major milestones.

name: behavior_git_governance
when: Creating branches, merging, coordinating reviews, mirroring repos.
steps: Review `docs/GIT_STRATEGY.md` for branching/messaging guardrails.
Create branches as `role/short-slug`, run `pre-commit`.
Include action IDs and behaviors in commit/PR descriptions.
Require cross-role review before merge.

name: behavior_orchestrate_cicd
when: Designing or updating CI/CD pipelines, deployment workflows.
steps: Reference `docs/AGENT_DEVOPS.md`, `docs/GIT_STRATEGY.md`.
Configure pipelines to run pre-commit, pytest, npm build, secret scanning.
Capture deployment telemetry linked to ActionService.
Coordinate secrets via `SECRETS_MANAGEMENT_PLAN.md`.

name: behavior_validate_financial_impact
when: Evaluating budget requests, ROI analyses, pricing impacts.
steps: Collect cost forecasts and telemetry baselines.
Model best/base/worst scenarios.
Validate against Finance guardrails.
Ensure financial telemetry is instrumented.

name: behavior_plan_go_to_market
when: Crafting launch plans, messaging frameworks, enablement kits.
steps: Map segments and personas to value propositions.
Align messaging across Web/API/CLI/MCP surfaces.
Inventory launch assets with owners and dates.
Define adoption KPIs and telemetry dashboards.

name: behavior_validate_accessibility
when: Designing or auditing user-facing workflows for accessibility.
steps: Run automated scans (axe, Lighthouse, PA11y).
Perform keyboard and screen reader walkthroughs.
Review copy for clarity and consistent tone.
Verify semantic markup and ARIA metadata.
Track remediation in dashboards.
